title: The Way To Behave.
content:
Though tempers are bad and peevish folks swear,
    Remember to ruffle thy brows, friend, ne'er;
    And let not the fancies of women so fair
    E'er serve thy pleasure in life to impair.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Johann Wolfgang von Goethe delivers a powerful performance in "The Way To Behave."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
